name: Montreal

actions.title: By-law for a Diverse Metropolis replaced with single 20% off-market housing obligation
actions.summary: Mayor Martinez Ferrada replaced the three-part 20-20-20 requirement with a single 20% off-market housing obligation, applying only to projects larger than 18,000 m2. The change aims to reduce regulatory barriers and accelerate housing construction.

actions.title: Temporary exemption for downtown office-to-residential conversions
actions.summary: Amendment to the Diverse Metropolis bylaw exempts downtown office-to-residential conversions from affordable housing obligations through December 31, 2026, encouraging adaptive reuse of vacant office space.

contextBlurb: Families in Montreal face vacancy rates hovering around 2%, with average two-bedroom rents near $1,200. The city has moved from a complex 20-20-20 inclusionary zoning framework to a simplified single 20% off-market obligation, trying to balance affordable housing targets with the pace of new construction.

name: Quebec

actions.title: Accessory dwelling unit bylaw (R.V.Q. 3294)
actions.summary: Permits secondary dwellings (garden suites, ADUs) on lots with detached single-family homes without requiring land subdivision. Conditions vary by neighbourhood and lot type.

contextBlurb: Quebec City residents face tightening supply despite relatively lower rents compared to Montreal. The city has begun allowing accessory dwelling units on single-family lots, a first step toward gentle densification in established neighbourhoods.

name: Laval
contextBlurb: Laval housing policy is tracked as part of Quebec coverage. Provincial legislation and local zoning changes are monitored in the tracker.

name: Gatineau
contextBlurb: Gatineau's proximity to Ottawa creates unique cross-border housing pressure. Many federal workers live in Gatineau for lower housing costs, but rising demand has pushed rents up steadily.

name: Sherbrooke
contextBlurb: Sherbrooke housing policy is tracked as part of Quebec coverage. Provincial legislation and local zoning changes are monitored in the tracker.

name: Longueuil
contextBlurb: Longueuil sits at the heart of Montreal's south shore suburbs. Housing demand continues to rise as families seek alternatives to Montreal's tighter market.

name: Francheville
contextBlurb: Trois-Rivieres and surrounding communities in the Francheville census division are tracked as part of Quebec housing coverage.

name: Levis
contextBlurb: Levis housing policy is tracked as part of Quebec coverage. The city sits across the St. Lawrence from Quebec City and shares its regional housing market pressures.

name: Le Fjord-du-Saguenay
contextBlurb: Saguenay and surrounding communities in the Fjord-du-Saguenay census division are tracked as part of Quebec housing coverage.

name: Les Moulins
contextBlurb: Terrebonne and Mascouche in the Les Moulins census division form part of Montreal's rapidly growing northern suburbs. Housing starts have risen but supply still trails demand.
